div.head: Mr. Portman to Mr. Seward.

opener.dateline: Legation of the United States in Japan, Yedo, December 6, 1865.

div.p: Sir: I have the honor to transmit herewith (No. 1) translation of a letter from the minister for foreign affairs, announcing that the Mikado’s ratification of the treaties was promulgated in all parts of this empire on the 1st instant.
I have the honor to be, sir, very respectfully, your most obedient servant,

closer.signed: A. L. C. PORTMAN, Chargé d’Affaires ad interim in Japan.

postscript.p: Hon. William H. Seward, Secretary of State, Washington, D. C.

frus:attachment.head: [Translation.]
frus:attachment.p: I have the honor to inform you that, on the 14th instant, (the 1st December,) all Daimios and others were notified that the treaties have been ratified by the Mikado.
Which I have to state with respect and esteem.

closer.dateline: On the seventeenth day of the tenth month of the first year of Ké Yu, (December 4.)

postscript.p: A. L. C. Portman, Esq., Chargé d’Affaires of the United States, &c., &c.
